user_query:
I just wrapped up a meeting about publishing trends and need a clean, standardized summary plus a quick metrics snapshot. Please do the following using only the files in this workspace:

1) Inspect input/notes and identify the most recent meeting notes file based on the date in the filename pattern YYYY-MM-DD_meeting_notes.md. Use only that latest file as the source. The directory contains multiple notes; do not hardcode a filename.

2) Rewrite the latest notes into a structured summary and save it as output/meeting_summary_<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (use the date from the chosen file). Include the following sections in this order (keep the section header even if no items are found):
- Title: "Meeting Summary — <YYYY-MM-DD>"
- Attendees: comma-separated list extracted from the line starting with "Attendees:" in the source notes
- Agenda: bullet list extracted from the Agenda section if present
- Decisions: bullet list of any lines beginning with "Decision:" (case-insensitive)
- Action Items: bullet list of any lines beginning with "Action:" (case-insensitive). If an owner appears in parentheses right after the name (e.g., "Priya (analytics)"), normalize the bullet as "[Owner] - task" where Owner is the person's name only (e.g., "Priya").
- Discussion Highlights: up to 5 concise bullets paraphrased from discussion content
- Metrics Snapshot: one short paragraph with the aggregated stats from input/publishing_metrics.csv for the last three calendar months including the meeting month (see #3). Also include a sentence with counts of the keywords "open access", "preprint", and "peer review" found in the raw notes (case-insensitive).

3) From input/publishing_metrics.csv, compute aggregates for the three-month period that includes the meeting month and the two previous months. Save a separate CSV at output/metrics_snapshot_<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv with columns:
- period_start (YYYY-MM)
- period_end (YYYY-MM)
- submissions_total (sum over the period)
- acceptance_rate (sum(acceptances)/sum(submissions), rounded to two decimals)
- oa_share_mean (mean of oa_share over the period, rounded to two decimals)
- avg_review_days_mean (mean of avg_review_days over the period, rounded to one decimal)
- meeting_date (YYYY-MM-DD)

4) In the Metrics Snapshot section of the summary markdown, include the above aggregates in a readable sentence (e.g., total submissions, acceptance_rate, oa_share_mean, avg_review_days_mean, and the period range). Also report the keyword mention counts ("open access", "preprint", "peer review") you computed from the raw notes text.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not use any external data. All inputs are local.
- Determining the latest notes file by scanning the directory is required.
- The meeting date for filenames and period selection must come from the chosen notes filename.
- Keep all sections in the markdown even if they end up empty.
- Overwrite output files if they already exist.

Inputs:
- input/notes (directory with dated notes files)
- input/publishing_metrics.csv (monthly metrics)

Outputs:
- output/meeting_summary_<YYYY-MM-DD>.md
- output/metrics_snapshot_<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
